channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In lawsuit filing, Pentagon says transgender troops can't serve unless they meet a warfighting need
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon revealed the specifics of its new transgender troop policy in a court filing Wednesday that says any service member or recruit who has been diagnosed with or treated for gender dysphoria is disqualified from serving — unless they can prove they meet a specific warfight
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigations into the Texas Lottery grow after courier controversy
item.description: AG Ken Paxton’s office joins two other investigations by state officials into the commission as one service suspends operations due to the agency’s ban.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Nevada lawmakers told Medicaid cuts could mean huge budget hole, widespread loss of benefits
item.description: With a key budget hearing on Medicaid funding scheduled on Thursday, Nevada lawmakers worked Wednesday to be prepared if federal cuts pose a threat to the state budget.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon orders new purge of social media sites to dump diversity, inclusion mentions by March 5
item.description: Building lethality in the military may be the buzzword for the new Trump administration, but busywork and paperwork have become the reality at the Pentagon, as service members and civilian workers are facing a broad mandate to purge all of the department’s social media sites and untangle confusing p
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to subject all contracts, grants through rigorous standards with DOGE executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday, implementing the cost efficiency initiative for DOGE, which looks for government wasteful spending.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VA pauses billions in cuts lauded by Musk as lawmakers and veterans decry loss of critical care
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs has temporarily suspended billions of dollars in contract cuts after an uproar that critical veterans health services were harmed, lawmakers and veterans service organizations said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp And Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dem bid to end Trump energy emergency squashed after forced Senate vote
item.description: Democrats in the Senate failed to pass a resolution that would have reversed President Donald Trump’s executive order on "Unleashing American Energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House unanimously passes bill to quadruple the pensions of Medal of Honor recipients
item.description: Legislation to quadruple pensions for Medal of Honor heroes passed the House Wednesday with strong bipartisan support and now goes to the United States Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge tells Trump administration it has less than 2 days to resume USAID funding
item.description: U.S. District Judge Amir H. Ali said officials have provided no evidence of compliance with repeated orders to unfreeze the money.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Should Kennedy seek to limit access to these medications, there are some avenues available to him in his new role.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Byron Donalds, backed by Trump, says he's running for Florida governor
item.description: Republican U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds announced Tuesday that he's entering the 2026 race to succeed Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, who is ineligible to seek another term after eight years in office.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Public records disputes target of Nevada lawmaker's bill creating an ombudsman
item.description: A tug-of-war lies ahead as groups look to influence legislation designed to resolve disputes over access to public records.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign, saying they won’t help ‘dismantle’ public services
item.description: Twenty-one members of the United States DOGE Service have resigned, they said in an anonymous letter, citing DOGE’s ongoing work dramatically reshaping the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5
